title: Code editor component with linked file support and conflict resolution
purpose: Provides a code editing interface that integrates with file system operations, handles conflicts between local edits and disk changes, and supports navigation features like function jumping and symbol references.
module_context: Exports a React functional component CodeEditor that wraps SnippetEditor for linked code files, managing state for push/sync operations, conflict detection, and navigation history.

steps.content: Calculate the time difference between current time and the provided timestamp.
Format the time difference as a human-readable relative time string based on the delta.
Return 'just now' for differences less than a minute.
Return minutes ago for differences less than an hour.
Return hours ago for differences less than a day.
Return days ago for differences greater than or equal to a day.

methods.name: parseLinkedEnvelope
methods.normalized_params: (content: string | undefined): { linked: true; code: string; language: string; filePath: string; dirty: boolean; lastPushedAt: number | null; lastSyncedAt: number; proposedEdit: { newCode: string; message?: string; proposedAt: number } | null } | null

steps.content: Check if the content is null or undefined and return null if so.
Attempt to parse the JSON content.
Verify that the parsed data has a linked field set to true.
Extract and validate the code, language, file path, dirty status, last pushed timestamp, and last synced timestamp from the parsed data.
Parse and validate the proposed edit data if present.
Return the parsed envelope data or null if parsing fails.
Extract the file name from the file path by splitting on forward slashes.
Find the last occurrence of a dot in the file name.
If a dot is found and it's not at the beginning or end of the file name, return the substring before the dot.
Otherwise, return the entire file name.

methods.name: buildLinkedSnippetRefs
methods.normalized_params: (snippets: Array<{ id: string; content: string | undefined }>): { id: string; filePath: string }[]

steps.content: Initialize an empty array to store the results.
Iterate through each snippet in the input array.
Attempt to parse the snippet content as JSON.
Check if the parsed data indicates a linked file and has a valid file path.
If conditions are met, add the snippet ID and file path to the results array.
Return the array of linked snippet references.
Initialize state variables for editor functionality including pushing, syncing, conflict detection, flash messages, and modal visibility.

Parse the linked envelope from the snippet content using a memoized approach.
Load functions for jump dropdown using pseudo-db lookup and fallback to regex extraction.

Implement navigation history handling for back button functionality.

Render the appropriate UI based on whether the snippet is linked or not.
Display proposed edit review banner if a proposed edit exists.
